ddls:
CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "username" TEXT /* Username chosen by the user */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the user */,
  "password" TEXT /* Hashed password for the user */,
  "first_name" TEXT /* First name of the user */,
  "last_name" TEXT /* Last name of the user */,
  "status" TEXT /* Status of the user account (e.g., active, suspended, locked) */,
  "last_login" TEXT /* Timestamp of the last login */,
  "failed_login_attempts" INTEGER /* Number of failed login attempts */,
  "account_locked_until" TEXT /* Timestamp until which the account is locked due to failed login attempts */,
  "is_admin" INTEGER /* Indicates if the user has admin privileges */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the user account was created */,
  "updated_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the user account was last updated */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "roles" (
  "role_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each role */,
  "role_name" TEXT /* Name of the role (e.g., admin, moderator, user) */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the role */,
  "is_system_role" INTEGER /* Indicates if the role is a system-defined role and cannot be deleted */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the role was created */,
  "updated_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the role was last updated */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("role_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "permissions" (
  "permission_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each permission */,
  "permission_name" TEXT /* Name of the permission (e.g., create_post, edit_post) */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the permission */,
  "is_system_permission" INTEGER /* Indicates if the permission is a system-defined permission and cannot be deleted */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the permission was created */,
  "updated_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the permission was last updated */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("permission_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "role_permissions" (
  "role_id" INTEGER /* ID of the role */,
  "permission_id" INTEGER /* ID of the permission */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the role-permission relationship was created */,
  "updated_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the role-permission relationship was last updated */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("role_id", "permission_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "user_roles" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user */,
  "role_id" INTEGER /* ID of the role */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the user-role relationship was created */,
  "updated_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the user-role relationship was last updated */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id", "role_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "activity_logs" (
  "log_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each log entry */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user */,
  "activity_type" TEXT /* Type of activity (e.g., login, logout) */,
  "activity_timestamp" TEXT /* Timestamp of the activity */,
  "ip_address" TEXT /* IP address from which the activity was performed */,
  "user_agent" TEXT /* User agent string of the device used */,
  "activity_details" TEXT /* Additional details about the activity */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("log_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_activity_logs_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "password_resets" (
  "reset_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each reset request */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user */,
  "reset_token" TEXT /* Password reset token */,
  "reset_timestamp" TEXT /* Timestamp of the reset request */,
  "expires_at" TEXT /* Timestamp until which the reset token is valid */,
  "is_used" INTEGER /* Indicates if the reset token has been used */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("reset_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_password_resets_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)
